page.content: In the first table, titled "Excluding antibody titer >10,000 mIU/mL," the time points and corresponding data are detailed as follows: at 6 weeks, there are 334 subjects with a seropositivity rate of 95.1%, a GMC of 64.9, and a 95% CI of 58.4–72.2; at 6 months, 290 subjects show a seropositivity rate of 97.9%, a GMC of 138.1, and a 95% CI of 119.2–147.1; at 12 months, 186 subjects have a seropositivity rate of 98.3%, a GMC of 135.2, and a 95% CI of 110.4–147.7; at 24 months, 133 subjects report a seropositivity rate of 96.2%, a GMC of 124.6, and a 95% CI of 96.2–152.9; at 36 months, 135 subjects demonstrate a seropositivity rate of 97.8%, a GMC of 137.6, and a 95% CI of 104.5–149.2; at 48 months, 102 subjects have a seropositivity rate of 92.6%, a GMC of 127.4, and a 95% CI of 91.3–155.1; at 60 months, 111 subjects show a seropositivity rate of 97.3%, a GMC of 127.1, and a 95% CI of 70.1–156.3.

page.content: The second table presents sex distribution across age cohorts, revealing that in the age cohort "up to 7 years," there are 5 girls and 1 boy, totaling 6 (14.6%); for "8 – 12 years," 5 girls and 5 boys total 10 (24.4%); "12 – 15 years" has 12 girls and 7 boys, totaling 19 (46.3%); "16 and older" shows 3 girls and 3 boys, totaling 6 (14.6%); overall, the total count is 25 girls (61%) and 16 boys (39%), aggregating to 41 (100%).
